title: 10 best herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. The tincture's ability to relax muscles and improve circulation also plays a crucial role in alleviating stiffness and improving range of motion. Additionally, highbush cranberry contains antioxidants that help to protect against cellular damage and promote healing, ultimately contributing to the tincture's effectiveness in addressing the chronic pain and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Highbush cranberry tincture helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and anodyne properties: The tincture's analgesic properties help relieve pain, while its anodyne properties reduce discomfort and numbness, making it a natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder pain.

Immune system modulation: Highbush cranberry's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response, which may contribute to reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling.

Fruits: The fruits of the highbush cranberry plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka at a ratio of one part herbs to two parts solvent.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles, label and date them, and store them in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected joint. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby minimizing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant effects help protect damaged tissues from further harm, allowing for faster recovery and reduced stiffness. By targeting these underlying causes of frozen shoulder, turmeric tinctures can provide natural relief and improve range of motion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved range of motion.

Pain-relieving property: Turmeric's analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties can help alleviate chronic pain and discomfort in the affected joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder, while also promoting overall joint health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Roots: Turmeric roots are used to make medicinal tinctures due to their high content of volatile oils, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate the discomfort and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality turmeric root and vodka at a ratio of one part turmeric to four parts vodka.
Dry the fresh turmeric roots in a low-temperature oven or dehydrator for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Grind dried turmeric into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one tablespoon of turmeric powder with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark bottle, discarding solids and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work together to decrease inflammation and relax tense muscles, allowing for a greater range of motion. Additionally, ginger's warming properties can help to increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of ginger tincture help to numb the pain and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder, providing relief from aching and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant: Ginger tincture's muscle-relaxing properties can help to reduce tension in the surrounding muscles and connective tissue, which can contribute to improved range of motion and reduced stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Rhyzomes: Ginger's rhyzomes are high in vitamins A and C, as well as minerals like potassium and magnesium, making them effective for reducing joint stiffness and improving circulation to the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root weighing about 2 pounds or 900 grams with roots intact.
Wash the ginger thoroughly with filtered water removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned ginger in a single layer on paper towels for several hours.
Chop the dried ginger into small pieces and soak them in 100 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part ginger to 5 parts vodka.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily then strain through cheesecloth and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The herbal extract has natural analgesic properties that can block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from discomfort and soreness. Additionally, devil's claw may also improve range of motion and flexibility by reducing inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, allowing for greater mobility and reduced stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Devil's claw tincture contains iridoid glycosides that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint.

Pain-relieving properties: The tincture's analgesic compounds, such as harpagoside, may help to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic shoulder pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Muscle relaxant properties: Devil's claw's muscle-relaxing properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and tension in the shoulder region, which is a common symptom of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in harpagoside, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for relieving pain and reducing swelling in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried harpagophytum procumbens root and stem, ensuring no additives or contaminants are present.
Combine the dried herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing for a 2:5 ratio by weight.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction and solubilization.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.

Transfer the resulting herbal devil's claw tincture to dark-glass bottles, labeling them with the date prepared and recommended dosage of 20-30 drops twice daily for optimal relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. When taken internally, salicin reduces inflammation and pain in the joints, muscles, and connective tissue, providing relief from the stiffness and limited range of motion associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, white willow's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce scarring and adhesions that can contribute to chronic pain and discomfort in the affected joint.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tincture contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to reduce swelling, ease stiffness, and protect the joint from further damage caused by frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and antispasmodic effects: White willow tincture's analgesic and antispasmodic properties help to relieve pain, reduce muscle spasms, and improve range of motion in individuals with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: White willow roots contain a higher concentration of salicin than other parts of the plant, making them an effective ingredient for treating chronic pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bark of salix alba in the summer or early fall when it's rich in salicin.
Grind 50 grams of dried white willow bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered white willow bark with 150 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint pain and stiffness. The capsaicin in bell peppers works to block the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants in bell pepper tinctures help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the formation of adhesions that can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like turmeric (Curcuma longa) and ginger (Zingiber officinale), which have anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin and gingerol, may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Herbs like willow bark (Salix alba) and white willow (Salix alba var. caerulea) contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

Muscle relaxant properties: Herbs like valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) and passionflower (Passiflora incarnata) have been traditionally used to calm muscle spasms and promote relaxation, which may be beneficial in reducing tension and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks of the bell pepper plant have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote joint health, making them a valuable ingredient in tinctures for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh capsicum annuum peppers and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the peppers into small pieces and combine them with 4 cups of vodka in a large glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the final herbal bell pepper tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and label it clearly, using within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it targets inflammation and reduces pain. The Boswellia serrata extract in frankincense has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stiffness and swelling, allowing for increased mobility and range of motion. Additionally, frankincense's analgesic and antispasmodic properties work to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort. As a natural and non-invasive solution, frankincense tinctures provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms without the need for surgery or prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant activity of frankincense helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms such as chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration.
Analgesic and sedative: Frankincense's analgesic and sedative properties can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata resin in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal bioactivity.
Clean and chop the harvested resin into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient solvent extraction.
Combine 10 grams of chopped resin with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks with occasional gentle shaking allowing solutes to precipitate and mingle.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and using the resulting tincture within one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected joint. The natural ingredients in arnica, such as thymol and flavonoids, work to relax muscles, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating stiffness and discomfort. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder by decreasing prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which contribute to inflammation.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of arnica tincture help alleviate pain and discomfort in the affected joint, making it an effective natural remedy for managing frozen shoulder pain.
Immune-stimulating: Arnica's immune-stimulating effects may help reduce the severity of frozen shoulder by increasing the body's natural defenses against inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Arnica roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Barks: The barks of the mountain arnica plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arnica flowers at peak bloom ensuring no pesticides or chemicals were used in their cultivation.
Dry arnica flowers using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind dried arnica flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder and strain through a mesh screen to remove any lumps.
Combine 100 grams of powdered arnica with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar allowing the mixture to steep for six weeks shaking daily.
Filter tincture through cheesecloth into another glass bottle discarding solids and label for storage in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at peak potency, ideally on sunny days.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dry place away from direct sunlight or heat sources.
Chop the dried material into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for at least 6 weeks to allow the tincture to steep and extract its active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, and transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it reduces muscle spasms, relaxes joints, and eases stiffness. Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with vodka in a sterilized glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the valerian to infuse its properties into the vodka.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to a clean dark glass bottle.
Store the valerian tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years taking 20-30 drops sublingually 3 times daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried withania somnifera roots and stems from reputable sources.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife ensuring uniform size.
Combine the chopped plant material with 190 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling to the top.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar twice daily to ensure efficient extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint. The tincture's ability to relax muscle spasms also alleviates stiffness and rigidity, allowing for increased mobility and range of motion. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or air drying method to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Prepare the tincture by combining 1 part dried ginkgo biloba with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and steeping for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and transfer it to a dark glass bottle, labeling it with ingredients and date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it targets the inflammation and discomfort associated with this condition. The beta-carotene and other antioxidants in saw palmetto work to reduce inflammation, which is a common characteristic of frozen shoulder. Additionally, saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) may help alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness by reducing the formation of scar tissue that can contribute to restricted mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Saw palmetto contains anti-inflammatory fatty acids that may help reduce inflammation in the affected shoulder joint, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain, making them a suitable choice for frozen shoulder treatment.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant contain fatty acids and phytosterols that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and stems from the wild or cultivate them in your garden for two weeks to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its essential oils and resins for maximum extraction efficiency.
Combine one part chopped plant material with three parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a maceration mixture, allowing it to steep for four hours.
Strain the maceration mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids and discarding the remaining plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, store them in a cool dark place for at least six weeks to allow for sedimentation before filtering and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that target the affected areas, reducing pain and stiffness. Barks: Containing flavonoids and lignans, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to relieve pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yucca schidigera roots in the spring or fall, ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested root material in a warm oven set at 150°f for 3-4 hours to preserve its bioactive compounds.
Chop 10 ounces of dried yucca schidigera root into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it two-thirds full.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, before straining and filtering the liquid.
Bottle and store the final tincture at room temperature, using 20-30 drops twice a day as needed, or as directed by a qualified healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the underlying inflammatory process. The tincture's potent anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. Additionally, hops' ability to modulate the body's stress response can help alleviate mental and emotional tension, which is often linked to frozen shoulder. By addressing both physical and emotional aspects of the condition, herbal hops tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Ant-inflammatory: Hops tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as humulone and lupulone, can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, thereby alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving: The tincture contains a small amount of myrcene, a natural analgesic that can help numb pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move the affected joint.
Antispasmodic: Hops tincture's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce stiffness in the shoulder region, which is often a hallmark symptom of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: Contains glycosides and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hops flowers from a trusted source and wash them gently under cold running water.
Combine 100g of dried hops flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking occasionally every other day.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Store the humulus lupulus tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder. These natural remedies help by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation, ultimately relieving pain and stiffness in the affected area. For example, tinctures containing devil's claw, turmeric, and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals with frozen shoulder can enjoy improved mobility and reduced pain, allowing them to resume daily activities and maintain a better quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal tinctures is to combine Arnica montana, with its anti-inflammatory properties, and Devil's Claw, which reduces pain and stiffness. Take a combination of 20-30 drops of each tincture three times daily, mixed with water or tea. Additionally, Turmeric, with its powerful anti-inflammatory compounds, can be taken in smaller doses to enhance the effectiveness of the treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions characterized by inflammation, stiffness, and limited mobility. Examples include tendinitis, bursitis, and carpal tunnel syndrome. Herbal tinctures like Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens) and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) help reduce pain and inflammation, while tinctures made from Willow Bark (Salix alba) and Ginger (Zingiber officinale) provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These natural remedies can be used in combination with physical therapy and other treatments to alleviate symptoms.
